messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Avoid Deceptive Data with Causal Roadmaps
messages.subject_clean: Avoid Deceptive Data with Causal Roadmaps
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

Enhancing the scientific validity of observational studies requires a disciplined approach to causal design. Data Can Deceive: Causal Roadmaps is a seminar livestreaming August 5, led by Kathryn Morrison (Precision Analytics) and Andy Wilson (Spencer Fox Eccles School of Medicine, University of Utah). This seminar provides a structured roadmap for moving from observational data to defensible causal estimands using target-trial emulation and directed acyclic graphs. Epidemiologists and R users can apply these rigorous design principles to their own datasets to eliminate common biases and ensure their statistical models reflect true causal processes rather than mere association. By integrating these workflows, researchers can produce highly credible, reproducible analyses that stand up to rigorous peer review.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/data-can-deceive-causal-roadmaps) to join the livestream, and please pass this along to colleagues and students who may find it useful!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Machine Learning for Computational Biology is enrolling
messages.subject_clean: Machine Learning for Computational Biology is enrolling
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

We are pleased to announce an upcoming livestream seminar designed to enhance your statistical and analytical capabilities. Machine Learning for Computational Biology 4.0 is a seminar livestreaming July 30, led by Nikolay Oskolkov (Lund University). This seminar provides researchers with the practical skills to code and implement machine learning algorithms from scratch in R and Python. Epidemiologists and biostatisticians can apply these advanced techniques to analyze complex genomic data and model intricate disease patterns. By mastering neural networks, random forests, and MCMC methods, you can significantly enhance the predictive power and analytical depth of your research projects.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/machine-learning-for-computational-biolo-4) to secure your spot, and please share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Last chance: Electoral Geography and Spatial Causal Inference
messages.subject_clean: Last chance: Electoral Geography and Spatial Causal Inference
messages.body_plain:
Hello R-SIG-EPI members

Last chance - Strengthen causal analysis of geographically structured epidemiological data. Electoral Geography & Spatial Causal Inference is a 2-day seminar livestreaming July 29?30 with Fernando De la Cuesta (Department of Applied, Public and Political Economics, Complutense University of Madrid). Compute distances and buffers, model spatial exposure, and assess residual spatial structure using R. Estimate local treatment effects at geographic boundaries with regression discontinuity designs and diagnostic checks. Apply these tools to epidemiological data involving place-based exposures, policy zones, and spatially structured health outcomes.

Sign up today (https://instats.org/seminar/electoral-geography-and-spatial-causal-i) to join before enrollment closes, and please pass this to colleagues and students who may be interested!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Instats | instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
